hero.title: 6 Days Nepal.
hero.titleAccent: Guided for Muslim Travellers from Malaysia.
hero.intro: All the wonder of Nepal — Kathmandu's ancient temples, Nagarkot's Himalayan sunrise, Pokhara's lake — with the Muslim-friendly logistics handled. Private tours, halal meal guidance, daily prayer times noted, and a team that understands what matters to you.
hero.heroImageAlt: Boudhanath Stupa Kathmandu Nepal with prayer flags

excludes: Nepal Visa on Arrival (USD30 for 15 days)
Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 6 Days Muslim-Friendly Nepal: The Full Picture
body: Nepal is not commonly thought of as a Muslim-friendly destination, but it absolutely can be — with the right preparation. Kathmandu has Indian and Middle Eastern restaurants with halal-certified kitchens, and Dhesu's team knows exactly where to find them. This package covers the same classic Nepal circuit as our Best of Nepal itinerary, with the additional Muslim-travel logistics handled: halal dining recommendations, prayer-time awareness and a private guide who respects your schedule.

Nepal is one of the world's most diverse spiritual landscapes — home to Hinduism and Buddhism in their most living, practised forms. For Muslim travellers, visiting the sacred sites of another faith with curiosity and open eyes is one of the most rewarding things travel can offer.

highlights.heading: Nepal for Muslim Travellers from Malaysia

items.title: Kathmandu — Ancient Faith, Open to All
items.body: Boudhanath Stupa, Pashupatinath Temple and Swayambhunath are places of living faith, not museums. They welcome respectful visitors from all backgrounds. Walking the kora at Boudhanath alongside monks and pilgrims is an experience that transcends any single religion.
items.imageAlt: Boudhanath Stupa Kathmandu Nepal circumambulation path

items.title: Nagarkot Sunrise — A Moment for Reflection
items.body: Standing on the Nagarkot ridge as the Himalayan arc turns gold in the first light of morning is one of those moments that makes the world feel very large and very beautiful. The pre-dawn stillness and the scale of the mountains create a natural space for gratitude.
items.imageAlt: Nagarkot sunrise Himalayan Nepal Muslim friendly

items.title: Pokhara — Serene and Family-Friendly
items.body: Pokhara's lakeside is one of the most relaxed places in Nepal — flat, walkable and calm. The Sarangkot sunrise over the Annapurna range, a boat ride on Phewa Lake and the World Peace Stupa across the water. Perfect pacing for families and groups.
items.imageAlt: Phewa Lake Pokhara Nepal family boat ride

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Kathmandu hotel. Evening at leisure — Thamel has excellent Indian and Middle Eastern halal restaurants.

days.title: Kathmandu UNESCO Temples
days.description: Full-day private tour with a Muslim-aware guide. Pashupatinath (viewed respectfully from the riverbank), Boudhanath Stupa and Swayambhunath. Prayer breaks built into the schedule.

days.title: Bhaktapur & Nagarkot Overnight
days.description: Morning in medieval Bhaktapur, then drive to Nagarkot for sunset and overnight at a mountain lodge.

days.title: Nagarkot Sunrise & Fly to Pokhara
days.description: Pre-dawn Himalayan sunrise at Nagarkot, then transfer to Pokhara. Afternoon at leisure by Phewa Lake.

days.title: Pokhara: Sarangkot & Lakeside
days.description: Early drive to Sarangkot for the Annapurna sunrise, followed by a relaxed lakeside morning. World Peace Stupa boat excursion.

days.title: Depart Pokhara or Kathmandu
days.description: Transfer to Pokhara Airport or Kathmandu for your international departure.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Hotels chosen for proximity to halal dining options and ease of finding qiblat direction. Breakfast halal-compliant.

tiers.label: Comfortable & Well-Located
tiers.hotel: 3-star Kathmandu + Nagarkot lodge + 3-star Pokhara lakeside or similar

tiers.hotel: 4-star Kathmandu + Nagarkot resort + 4-star Pokhara lakeside or similar

whyBook.heading: Muslim-Friendly Travel, Handled with Respect
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been serving Malaysian Muslim travellers since 1988. We know where to find certified halal kitchens in Kathmandu and Pokhara, and our guides understand the importance of prayer times and dietary requirements. Nepal is extraordinary — we make it accessible.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through Nepal Visa on Arrival documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours Only
points.body: No group coaches. Your private guide respects your prayer schedule and adapts the day accordingly.

points.title: Malaysian Team You Can Call
points.body: We speak Bahasa Malaysia. Reach us before, during and after your trip.

points.title: Group & Family Pricing
points.body: Larger groups mean lower per-person costs. Ask us for a family or group quote.

sections.heading: Muslim Friendly Nepal Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
Nepal is one of Asia's most spiritually rich destinations, and Malaysian Muslim travellers are discovering it in growing numbers. The country is welcoming, the people are warm, and with the right preparation the halal food question is entirely manageable.

Kathmandu has a significant Indian and South Asian expat community, which means Indian halal restaurants are plentiful in Thamel and Lazimpat. Pokhara's lakeside also has halal and vegetarian options. Our team provides a curated list of verified halal options with every booking.

sections.heading: Prayer Times and Qiblat Direction in Nepal
sections.body: Prayer times in Kathmandu and Pokhara follow the local Nepal time zone (UTC+5:45), and our guides build prayer breaks into the sightseeing schedule as a matter of course. Hotels have qiblat direction information available on request. Dhesu's team provides Subuh to Isyak prayer times for your specific travel dates with every booking confirmation.

items.question: Is halal food available in Nepal?
items.answer: Yes. Kathmandu has a well-established Indian Muslim restaurant scene in Thamel and the surrounding areas, with certified halal kitchens. Pokhara's lakeside also has vegetarian and halal options. Dhesu provides a curated list of verified halal dining options for each city as part of every Muslim-friendly booking.

items.question: Are there mosques in Kathmandu?
items.answer: Yes. The Jama Masjid (Kashmiri Mosque) near Durbar Marg in Kathmandu is the main Friday mosque. There are also smaller masjids in the Thamel area. Dhesu provides the nearest prayer facilities for each hotel we use.

items.question: Can we visit Nepal's temples as Muslim travellers?
items.answer: Most Buddhist sites (Boudhanath, Swayambhunath) are open to all visitors regardless of faith. Pashupatinath Temple (Hindu) restricts entry to its main temple for non-Hindus, but the sacred ghats and surrounding complex can be visited and observed respectfully from designated viewpoints — this is included in the tour. Our guide provides full cultural context for all sites.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: What is the best time for this package?
items.answer: October to December (autumn) and March to May (spring) offer the clearest Himalayan views. These are also Eid travel periods — book early if you plan to travel during Hari Raya, as Nepal is increasingly popular with Malaysian groups.
